paper.title_en: Shape coexistence in <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Cr</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mrow><mn>52</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>54</mn></mrow></mmultiscripts></math> and debate on the <math><msub><mi>B</mi><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo>/</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> anomaly in <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Cr</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>52</mn></mmultiscripts></math>
paper.abstract_en: The lowest states of the <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Cr</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mrow><mn>52</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>54</mn></mrow></mmultiscripts></math> isotopes are investigated for the presence of the shape coexistence phenomenon. The ground state deformation is extracted using the covariant density functional theory with a density-dependent point-coupling X parametrization, while the properties of the excited states are described involving the Bohr-Mottelson collective Hamiltonian with a sixth order anharmonic oscillator. A coexistence between near-spherical and well-deformed shapes is found in <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Cr</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>52</mn></mmultiscripts></math>, and shape fluctuations of the ground state in <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Cr</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>54</mn></mmultiscripts></math>. Moreover, the presence of coexisting shapes in <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Cr</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>52</mn></mmultiscripts></math> indicates that the <math><msup><mn>4</mn><mo>+</mo></msup></math> state at 2768 keV would fit better the ground band than the <math><msup><mn>4</mn><mo>+</mo></msup></math> state at 2370 keV. This latter assumption, in turn, implies that <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Cr</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>52</mn></mmultiscripts></math> could be a good candidate for the manifestation of the so-called <math><msub><mi>B</mi><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo>/</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> anomaly.

reasons: 未达到主题关键词门槛
